# robots.txt — file for controlling search engine crawlers (bots)
# Placed in the root of the site: https://your-domain.com/robots.txt

# Applies rules to all crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.)
User-agent: *

# Allow crawling of all pages on the site
# This means bots are permitted to access any URL
Allow: /

# IMPORTANT:
# This file ONLY controls crawling (access), NOT indexing.
# Even if a page is allowed here, it can still be excluded from search results
# using <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> in the HTML.

# For staging/development environments:
# It is common to allow crawling but prevent indexing via meta robots.
# This lets bots see the page but not include it in search results.

# In production, you typically also add a sitemap reference:
# Sitemap: https://your-domain.com/sitemap.xml

# Example of blocking specific sections (not used here):
# Disallow: /admin/
# Disallow: /api/

# WARNING:
# Do NOT use "Disallow: /" unless you intentionally want to block all crawling.
# If you block crawling, bots will not see meta tags like "noindex",
# and pages might still remain indexed based on external signals.

# Summary:
# - Allow: / → full crawl access
# - Indexing is controlled separately via meta robots or HTTP headers
